item.title: AI-Assisted Knowledge Management Seed
item.description: AI-Assisted Knowledge Management Seed Drop this file into any vault to bootstrap a healthy, self-improving knowledge base.

item.title: CLAUDE
item.description: AI-Assisted Knowledge Management Vault Purpose This vault develops and refines a universal “Seed” — a portable ruleset that lets an AI agent build a high-quality knowledge base on ANY topic from scratch.

item.title: Frontier Exploration - Knowledge Attention Signals
item.description: Frontier Exploration: Knowledge Attention Signals How should a knowledge base signal which notes need immediate attention without requiring manual tracking? The Problem The Seed has comprehensive lifecycle tracking (draft → active → evergreen → deprecated → archived) and review date tracking (last-r...

item.title: Frontier Exploration - Knowledge Debt
item.description: Frontier Exploration - Knowledge Debt Managing the maintenance burden created by shortcuts and imperfect knowledge capture The Problem Just as software accumulates technical debt from quick-and-dirty solutions, knowledge bases accumulate knowledge debt — shortcuts, compromises, and incomplete captur...

item.title: _root
item.description: Knowledge Base Vault “Knowledge is only as valuable as the connections it enables.” Purpose This vault demonstrates AI-Assisted Knowledge Management — an AI agent autonomously collects, processes, and structures data into atomic knowledge nodes connected in an interconnected graph.

item.title: Graph Maintenance
item.description: Graph Maintenance Regular maintenance keeps the knowledge base healthy and navigable.

item.title: Seed Stress Test - Graph Density Rule in Genealogy
item.description: Seed Stress Test: Graph Density Rule in Genealogy Knowledge Base Testing the “Graph density (edges/nodes ratio) should increase over time” rule against genealogy/family history domain reveals unique challenges.

item.title: Active Reading
item.description: Active Reading Active reading means engaging deeply with knowledge base content rather than passively consuming it. The Three Pillars 1.

item.title: Advanced Pattern Matching
item.description: Advanced Pattern Matching Using graph analysis to detect structural patterns and health issues in knowledge bases.

item.title: Anti-Pattern - Capture-Process Confusion
item.description: Anti-Pattern: Capture-Process Confusion Trying to fully process notes at capture time breaks the workflow.
